examples: Two opposing scenarios, the "arboreal" hypothesis and the "cursorial" hypothesis, have traditionally been put forward concerning the origins of bird flight.
Even with snow on the ground, the high bushy understory is exposed; also snow and wind bring down leafy branches of cedar, hemlock, red alder, and other arboreal fodder.
In one, cover was created with eucalyptus branches and light levels were kept low, mimicking an arboreal habitat.
Selection on an arboreal ape population will be impotent if variations which improve their terrestrial prospects are sparse.
